title: Global Health Agencies Respond to Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship
subtitle: As a cruise ship carrying sick passengers approaches the Canary Islands, experts question the changing role of the U.S. government in international health emergencies.

summary.en: A cruise ship named MV Hondius is currently at the center of a hantavirus outbreak that has resulted in three deaths. The World Health Organization is leading the international response to manage the ship's arrival in Spain. Meanwhile, the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has faced criticism for its slow and limited involvement. Experts worry that the U.S. is no longer as prepared for global health threats as it once was. American passengers are expected to be evacuated to a military base in Nebraska for monitoring.
summary.zh: 一艘名為「宏迪斯號」的郵輪目前正處於漢他病毒疫情的中心，該疫情已造成三人死亡。世界衛生組織正主導國際應對措施，以管理該船抵達西班牙的事宜。與此同時，美國疾病管制暨預防中心因反應遲緩且參與有限而面臨批評。專家擔心美國對全球衛生威脅的準備程度已不如從前。美國籍乘客預計將被撤離至內布拉斯加州的一處軍事基地接受監控。

paragraphs.en: The cruise ship MV Hondius is currently the focus of a serious international health effort after a hantavirus outbreak was reported on board. Hantavirus is a rare but dangerous disease that can cause severe breathing problems. According to the World Health Organization (WHO), the outbreak began early last month when a passenger became ill during a trip from Argentina toward Antarctica. Since then, three people have died, and several others have shown signs of illness.
paragraphs.zh: 郵輪「宏迪斯號 (MV Hondius)」目前正處於一項重大國際衛生行動的核心，起因是船上傳出了漢他病毒疫情。漢他病毒是一種罕見但危險的疾病，可能導致嚴重的呼吸問題。根據世界衛生組織 (WHO) 的說法，疫情始於上個月初，當時一名乘客在從阿根廷前往南極洲的旅途中發病。自那時起，已有三人死亡，另有數人出現染病跡象。

paragraphs.en: As the ship travels toward the Canary Islands in Spain, the WHO has taken the lead in managing the situation. WHO Director-General Tedros recently addressed the people of Tenerife, the island where the ship is expected to arrive. He explained that while the situation is serious, the risk to the local public is low. He noted that the ship will dock at an industrial port far from residential areas, and passengers will be moved in guarded vehicles to ensure they do not come into contact with the local population. The WHO emphasized that this plan follows international rules, which require the nearest port with enough medical equipment to help those in need.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著該船駛向西班牙的加那利群島，世衛組織已主導處理此一情況。世衛組織秘書長譚德塞 (Tedros) 近日向該船預計抵達的特內里費島居民發表談話。他解釋說，儘管情況嚴重，但對當地民眾的風險很低。他指出，郵輪將停靠在遠離住宅區的工業港口，乘客將被轉移至有警衛看守的車輛中，以確保他們不會與當地居民接觸。世衛組織強調，此計畫遵循國際規則，該規則要求必須停靠在擁有足夠醫療設備以協助患者的最近港口。

paragraphs.en: While the WHO coordinates the arrival, the response from the United States has caused significant debate. The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) confirmed it is sending a team to meet American passengers upon their arrival in Spain. These passengers will then be taken to an airbase in Nebraska for medical evaluation and monitoring. Despite these actions, many health experts are concerned that the CDC has been slow to react. According to The Guardian Health, the CDC did not activate its emergency center until several days after the outbreak was reported to the WHO.
paragraphs.zh: 在世衛組織協調船隻抵達的同時，美國的反應引發了廣泛爭論。美國疾病管制暨預防中心 (CDC) 確認將派遣團隊在西班牙迎接美國籍乘客。這些乘客隨後將被送往內布拉斯加州的一處空軍基地進行醫療評估與監控。儘管採取了這些行動，許多衛生專家仍擔心美國疾管中心反應遲緩。根據《衛報健康版 (The Guardian Health)》報導，直到疫情通報給世衛組織數日後，美國疾管中心才啟動其緊急應變中心。

paragraphs.en: Lawrence Gostin, an expert in public health at Georgetown University, told STAT News that the CDC’s limited role is surprising. He noted that in the past, the CDC was always a primary leader in solving international health mysteries. However, in this case, the agency seems to be taking a backseat. Dr. Jeanne Marrazzo, head of the Infectious Diseases Society of America, described the situation as a "sentinel event," meaning it is a warning sign that the country may not be as prepared for future disease threats as it needs to be.
paragraphs.zh: 喬治城大學 (Georgetown University) 的公共衛生專家勞倫斯·高斯汀 (Lawrence Gostin) 向《STAT新聞》表示，美國疾管中心角色受限令人驚訝。他指出，過去美國疾管中心總是在解決國際衛生謎團時扮演主要領導角色。然而在此案中，該機構似乎退居幕後。美國傳染病學會 (Infectious Diseases Society of America) 會長珍妮·馬拉佐 (Jeanne Marrazzo) 醫師將此情況描述為「哨兵事件」，意指這是一個警訊，顯示該國對於未來疾病威脅的準備程度可能不如預期。

paragraphs.en: In response to these concerns, Dr. Jay Bhattacharya of the National Institutes of Health stated that the CDC has been coordinating with international partners from the beginning. He explained that the agency is providing its technical knowledge to help with the safe return of American citizens. However, even with these assurances, the way the U.S. is handling the crisis remains a point of tension. During a recent briefing, officials promised to be open with the public but required that media representatives not name the speakers, a rule that some found unusual for a public health agency.
paragraphs.zh: 針對這些擔憂，美國國家衛生研究院 (National Institutes of Health) 的傑伊·巴塔查里亞 (Jay Bhattacharya) 醫師表示，美國疾管中心從一開始就與國際夥伴保持協調。他解釋說，該機構正提供技術知識，以協助美國公民安全返國。然而，即便有這些保證，美國處理危機的方式仍是緊張的焦點。在最近的一場簡報中，官員承諾會對大眾公開透明，卻要求媒體代表不得公開發言者的姓名，這項規定讓一些人認為對於一個公共衛生機構來說並不尋常。

paragraphs.en: For many, this outbreak highlights a shift in how the U.S. interacts with global health organizations. The U.S. is no longer a member of the WHO, which has changed how the two organizations work together. In previous decades, the CDC and the WHO were close partners, sharing staff and information to stop the spread of diseases. Today, the WHO is managing the ship’s arrival while the U.S. government focuses on its own citizens through separate diplomatic and military channels.
paragraphs.zh: 對許多人而言，此次疫情凸顯了美國與全球衛生組織互動方式的轉變。美國已不再是世衛組織的成員，這改變了兩個組織之間的合作模式。在過去幾十年裡，美國疾管中心與世衛組織是密切的夥伴，透過共享人員與資訊來阻止疾病傳播。如今，世衛組織負責管理郵輪的抵達，而美國政府則透過獨立的外交與軍事管道專注於照顧本國公民。

paragraphs.en: As the MV Hondius nears the end of its journey, the focus remains on the safety of the passengers and the local community. The captain of the ship and the operating company have been praised by the WHO for their cooperation during this difficult time. While the immediate danger of the virus is being managed, the debate over the U.S. government’s response is likely to continue. Experts warn that if the country does not return to its former role as a leader in global health, it may struggle to handle larger or more dangerous outbreaks in the future. For now, the world is watching to see how the final stages of the evacuation proceed and whether the current health measures will be enough to keep everyone safe.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著宏迪斯號接近旅程終點，焦點仍放在乘客與當地社區的安全上。船長與營運公司在這次艱難時期展現的配合度，受到了世衛組織的讚揚。雖然病毒帶來的直接危險正受到控制，但關於美國政府反應的爭論可能會持續下去。專家警告，如果該國無法重回過去在全球衛生領域的領導地位，未來在應對更大或更危險的疫情時可能會陷入困境。目前，全世界都在關注撤離行動的最後階段將如何進行，以及現行的衛生措施是否足以確保所有人的安全。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be the person or group in control of a situation or project.
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語表示承擔領導責任，而非字面上的「拿走領先」。文中：As the ship travels toward the Canary Islands in Spain, the WHO has taken the lead in managing the situation.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To choose to be less active or less important in a situation.
keyTerms.noteZh: 源自汽車後座，比喻退居幕後，不再擔任主導角色。文中：However, in this case, the agency seems to be taking a backseat.

keyTerms.definitionEn: An unexpected occurrence that serves as a warning sign of a larger problem.
keyTerms.noteZh: sentinel 原指「哨兵」，這裡指具有警示作用的事件。文中：Dr. Jeanne Marrazzo, head of the Infectious Diseases Society of America, described the situation as a "sentinel event," meaning it is a warning sign that the country may not be as prepared for future disease threats as it needs to be.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: ‘Empty and vapid’ CDC finally responds to hantavirus outbreak. But experts say it’s too little, too late

sources.name: STAT News
sources.title: Experts wonder ‘Where is the CDC?’ as hantavirus outbreak unfolds

sources.name: WHO News
sources.title: Message by the WHO Director-General to the people of Tenerife regarding the hantavirus response

quiz.question: What specific measure is being taken to protect the residents of Tenerife from the MV Hondius passengers?

options.A: The ship is being diverted to a different island further away from populated areas.
options.B: Passengers will be transported through guarded vehicles to an industrial port.
options.C: The local population is being evacuated from Tenerife before the ship arrives.
options.D: The ship will remain at sea until all passengers have recovered from the virus.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，WHO 為了保護居民，安排船隻停靠在遠離住宅區的工業港口，並使用受保護的車輛運送乘客，以確保他們不會接觸到當地民眾。選項 A 錯誤，因為船隻確實會抵達特內里費島；選項 C 錯誤，文中並未提到撤離當地居民；選項 D 錯誤，文中提到的是管理風險與醫療協助，而非無限期留在海上。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the current relationship between the U.S. government and the WHO regarding this health crisis?

options.A: They are working as a single, unified team to manage the ship's arrival.
options.B: The U.S. is providing the majority of the medical funding for the WHO's operation.
options.C: They are operating through separate channels due to changes in their formal partnership.
options.D: The U.S. is intentionally blocking the WHO from accessing the passengers on the ship.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第六段，由於美國已不再是 WHO 的成員，兩者的合作方式已改變，美國政府目前透過獨立的外交與軍事管道處理其公民的撤離，而非與 WHO 共同管理。選項 A 錯誤，兩者運作方式分離；選項 B 文中未提及資金問題；選項 D 錯誤，文中並未提到美國阻撓 WHO 的行動。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the term 'sentinel event' mean in the context of Dr. Marrazzo's statement?

options.A: A minor incident that is easily resolved by experts.
options.B: A warning sign indicating a lack of preparedness for future threats.
options.C: A historical moment that marks the end of a disease outbreak.
options.D: A successful strategy used to prevent the spread of infections.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文中提到 Dr. Marrazzo 將此事件稱為「sentinel event」，並緊接著解釋這是一個「warning sign」（警示訊號），代表美國在面對未來疾病威脅時可能準備不足。選項 A 與 D 與「警告」的語意相反；選項 C 錯誤，這並非指事件的結束，而是指對未來風險的啟示。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the MV Hondius outbreak?

options.A: The outbreak is a minor health issue that has been fully resolved by the cruise company.
options.B: The U.S. government's response has been perfect and serves as a model for other nations.
options.C: The situation highlights concerns about the U.S.'s changing role and effectiveness in global health crises.
options.D: The main priority of the article is to criticize the passengers for traveling during a pandemic.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章不僅報導了疫情，更花費大量篇幅討論美國 CDC 反應遲緩、與 WHO 的合作關係改變，以及專家擔心美國若不再擔任全球衛生領導者，未來將難以應對更嚴重的疫情。選項 A 忽略了文章對美國反應的討論；選項 B 與文章觀點相反；選項 D 並非文章重點，文章並未指責乘客。
